page: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, featuring 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Storage is provided via 1 GB of network-attached storage, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance alongside average multi-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the server achieves top-tier results in the top 10% category, which is mirrored by top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core CPU metrics, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark database operations falling into the average middle 50% range. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable bottleneck, registering in the weak bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently rank in the average tier, as do Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU resources. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, vector neural network instructions (AVX512_VNNI), and SHA extensions, which assist in cryptography and basic vector math. While small LLM inference tasks perform at average speeds, larger models like the 7B parameter model experience poor prompt processing performance. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, lightweight database operations, and single-threaded utility tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, 1 GB of network storage, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. The processor features a 32 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and cryptography extensions. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Multi-threaded decompression represents a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. This shared-CPU instance provides a cost-effective profile for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and lightweight database operations that benefit from strong single-threaded performance without requiring dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-4c-16g is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage, offering strong single-core performance for balanced workloads.

tagline: A balanced shared-CPU virtual instance featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance and sixteen gigabytes of memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 4 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
1 GB of network-attached local storage and one public IPv4 address
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and SHA cryptography extensions
Best suited for static web serving, lightweight databases, and general-purpose tasks
